behaviors.description: The speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels so that the summary can attribute statements to specific attendees.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is real STT diarization quality/content, not just local wiring. A stubbed transcript with perfect labels would hide real misses (MOCK-HIDE). No required multi-agent sequence is stated beyond this model-dependent output, so Workflow is not required.
behaviors.test_description: Run with a real meeting audio sample against the real speech-to-text API and assert the produced transcript includes correct speaker-labeled utterances that can be mapped to attendee identities for downstream summarization.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Rejected Workflow: behavior text focuses on single STT output property ("speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels"), not multiple agentic decisions in sequence.

behaviors.description: Transcript parsing extracts explicit task assignments (assignee + task text) into the summary input structure.
behaviors.justification: Behavior targets deterministic parsing/structuring logic inside one boundary. Real OS/network/model output is not required to expose this failure; crafted transcript fixtures are sufficient.
behaviors.test_description: Feed parser function transcript text fixtures containing explicit assignments and assert extracted records include assignee and task text fields in the summary-input schema.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Rejected Integration: behavior text is local extraction logic ("Transcript parsing extracts...") without required cross-module/infra boundary.

behaviors.description: The live summary output contains at least one action item for every attendee who was assigned a task during the meeting.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real LLM output quality/completeness (coverage by attendee). Deterministic mocks can always satisfy coverage and hide real omissions. This is one model-output quality check, not an explicitly multi-agent chain.
behaviors.test_description: Use real transcript inputs with known attendee-task assignments, call the real summarization LLM, and assert each assigned attendee appears in at least one generated action item.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Rejected Workflow: behavior token "live summary output" specifies one output quality criterion, not a required multi-step agent pipeline.

behaviors.description: Decision extraction logic routes identified decisions into the `decisions` section schema rather than generic narrative fields.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is routing/mapping logic after decisions are already identified. The text scopes to local schema placement, so a single-function/object test can catch real failures.
behaviors.test_description: Given an input object containing pre-identified decision items, assert mapper output places them under `decisions` and not narrative fields.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Rejected Agentic: phrase "identified decisions" disqualifies dependence on model decision quality; this test starts after identification.

behaviors.description: Key decisions mentioned in the transcript appear in the live summary's `decisions` section, not buried in the narrative.
behaviors.justification: This validates model extraction/placement quality from natural language transcript content. Stubs would mask whether the real LLM actually surfaces key decisions in the right section.
behaviors.test_description: Run real transcripts containing known decision statements through the real summarizer and assert those decisions are present in `decisions` section with acceptable recall, not only narrative text.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Rejected Workflow: behavior asks for a single end-output property (decision presence/location), not explicitly multiple agentic handoffs.

behaviors.description: When the audio file is longer than 60 minutes, the service splits it into segments and processes them sequentially, merging the results into a single coherent summary.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires sequenced processing and merge across segments (cross-step state/order), plus coherence of final summary, which depends on chained model outputs. This is multi-step agentic orchestration by definition.
behaviors.test_description: Execute end-to-end with a real >60-minute recording: verify segment boundaries and sequential processing order, then validate merged summary coherence and schema completeness across segment-spanning topics/actions/decisions.

behaviors.description: The full pipeline (upload audio, transcribe, summarize, deliver structured output) completes without manual intervention for a real meeting recording.
behaviors.justification: This is explicitly full-pipeline, real recording, and multiple model-dependent stages in sequence. Lower levels miss cross-stage failures, handoff/state issues, and emergent failures across transcribe→summarize→delivery.
behaviors.test_description: Run a real meeting audio through production-like pipeline (real upload path, real STT, real LLM summarization, real output delivery endpoint) and assert unattended completion with valid structured output and no stuck/recovery-required step.
